chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَنْ يَهُمُّ بِالْحَسَنَةِ أَوْ السَّيِّئَةِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 190 – The one who intends performing the good deed or the evil deed

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/292)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/292)
text: إِنَّ الْمُؤْمِنَ لَيَهُمُّ بِالْحَسَنَةِ وَ لَا يَعْمَلُ بِهَا فَتُكْتَبُ لَهُ حَسَنَةٌ وَ إِنْ هُوَ عَمِلَهَا كُتِبَتْ لَهُ عَشْرُ حَسَنَاتٍ وَ إِنَّ الْمُؤْمِنَ لَيَهُمُّ بِالسَّيِّئَةِ أَنْ يَعْمَلَهَا فَلَا يَعْمَلُهَا فَلَا تُكْتَبُ عَلَيْهِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Abu Abdullah, from usman Bin isa, from Sama’at Bin Mihran, from Abu Baseer,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘The Momin intends the good deed and does not do it, so one good deed is Written for him; and if he were to do it, ten good deeds would be Written for him; but if the Momin intends the evil deed that he would be doing it, but he does not do it, so it would not be Written against him’.
en.sarwar: 2. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn abu ‘Abd Allah from ‘Uthman ibn ‘Isa from Sama’a ibn Mehran from abu Basir from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “A believer may intend to perform a good deed but does not do it, still (the reward for) one good deed will be written for him and if he actually completes it, (the reward for) ten good deeds will be written in his favor. A believer may intend to commit an evil deed but if he does not do it, nothing will be written against him.”
